title: rosa tomentosa var. marcyana
intro: Rosa tomentosa var. marcyana, also known as the woolly rose, is a fragrant and medicinal variety of rose that belongs to the family Rosaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, and also exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. Rosa tomentosa var. marcyana has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and promoting digestion, due to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its medicinal properties.
